title: The Invocation — Opening of Contemplation
body:
# The Invocation — Opening of Contemplation

"Toward the One, the perfection of love, harmony and beauty, the only being, united with all of the illuminated souls who form the embodiment of the messenger, the spirit of guidance."

The Invocation opens every Suluk session, marking the transition from ordinary awareness into sacred space. It is the first act of contemplative practice — gathering scattered attention into unified intention.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Aman and Dar al-Aman — The Realm of Sanctuary
body:
# Aman and Dar al-Aman — The Realm of Sanctuary

Aman isn't just something that arises outside — it arises outside because it begins in the interior life. The intention is to create the Dar al-Aman, the realm of sanctuary, beginning in our own hearts.

## Building Sanctuary

We do this through:
- The consideration that we have for all of God's creatures
- The consideration that we show to each other in Suluk
- The intention to make every thought, every word, and every action a stone in that temple of sanctuary that we're building

"We can't control what happens throughout the world, but we can create sanctuary amongst those we know, those we meet through the attitude of Aman."

Pir Zia acknowledges the suffering in the world — polarization, militarism, destruction — and holds prayer for those in conflict zones. Humanity is one family; what happens to the least protected happens to all of us.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: From Concentration to Contemplation — The Four Elements
body:
# From Concentration to Contemplation — The Four Elements

The four stages of Suluk study correspond to the four elements:

| Stage | Arabic | Element | Quality |
|-------|--------|---------|--------|
| **Concentration** | Mujahada | Earth | Heavy, dense, slow — learning to be still like a rock |
| **Contemplation** | Muraqabah | Water | The mind begins to move gracefully, artfully, purposefully |
| **Meditation** | — | Air | (To come) |
| **Realization** | — | Fire | (To come) |

## Earth — The Teaching of the Stone

"In our fast-moving world where attention is so rare, the very first medicine is to slow down and be like a rock. Just learn to be fully still, fully silent, fully present."

Rocks aren't usually given much consideration, but a rock can teach us much. A stone carries an abiding vibration because its consciousness isn't scattered — it's very rooted, very stable. There is a special place in California called Pir Dahan (the mouth of the Pir) where Hazrat Inayat Khan sat and meditated on a stone. You can still feel his vibrations there.

## Water — The Mind Begins to Move

Having been still and focused on one object, now the mind begins to move — not erratically, not randomly, but gracefully, artfully, purposefully. This is Muraqabah.

In contemplation we attend not to objects or forms, but to something subtler: the quality of something, the character, the nature — leading into the architecture of meaning that underlies the visible universe.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: The Divine Names — Living Anatomy of God's Attributes
body:
# The Divine Names — Living Anatomy of God's Attributes

The qualities of being that all things manifest are, in their essence, the attributes of God. The names of God invoke them. 99 is sometimes mentioned as a symbolic number, but there are more than 99.

## The Organic Nature of the Names

The names form a living anatomy — related to each other, branching out from each other, interacting like the organs of a living organism. Some names are more original; others unfold from those most primal names.

## Three Categories

| Category | Quality | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| **Jalali** | Majesty & Power | Names of divine might and sovereignty |
| **Jamali** | Beauty & Elegance | Names of divine grace and tenderness |
| **Kamali** | Beauty & Power merged | Names of divine perfection and completion |

## The Seven Leading Names (Al-Asma as-Sab'a al-A'imma)

Also called the **Ummahat** — the mother names, the matrices. Each gives rise to the next in an unfolding sequence that mirrors:
- The manner in which the universe came into being
- The process by which each of us came into being
- The way the fertilized egg develops, the process of birth and growth

The microcosmic process of emergence is embedded within and reflects the macrocosmic process of creation.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Wazifa — The Practice of the Divine Name
body:
# Wazifa — The Practice of the Divine Name

Wazifa means an assignment — a divine name entrusted to you as a daily practice. When you receive a Wazifa, you enter the field of that name. You dedicate your life to beholding God through the portal of this particular name. A door is opened to you. You become the 'abd — the servant of that name.

## Murshid's Teachings on Wazifa

> "Every spoken word is a re-echo of thought and every thought is the re-echo of an emotion. Wazifa is a voluntary production of a certain thing on the mind and in the heart."

### Principles of Practice

| Principle | Guidance |
|-----------|----------|
| **Numbers** | Follow the law of numbers — set count, regular rhythm; without consideration of numbers, rhythm and balance are lost |
| **Moon phase** | Commence during the waxing moon (progressive trend); waning moon brings upset and confusion |
| **Place** | Same place every day creates a growing atmosphere |
| **Time** | Same time every day — invisible forces get accustomed to meet and help |
| **Purity** | Cleanliness of body, purity of mind, fragrance, beautiful thoughts give quicker result |
| **Diet** | Flesh food is sometimes undesirable for Wazifa |
| **Secrecy** | Keep the Wazifa secret and its effect secret |
| **Authorization** | Must be given by a blessed soul or permission taken from a holy person — produces a thousandfold effect |

### The Process

The word becomes thought → thought becomes feeling → body, mind, heart, soul all become aligned in the field of the divine quality. With consistency, the magnetism builds. Angels and benevolent jinn join the practice. The name becomes a world.

"Weakness of character and an impious life, instead of bringing a good result, ruins the life of the one who repeats Wazifa."

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Al-Hayy — The Living (First Mother Name)
body:
# Al-Hayy — The Living (First Mother Name)

Al-Hayy is the first of the seven Ummahat (mother names). Why first? Before something can be or do anything, it must simply exist — live. Being, living, comes first. The universe begins with it.

## Mantric Language

Al-Hayy exemplifies mantric language — words and names whose very sonic structure embodies the quality they designate. Even without knowing the meaning, reciting "Hayy, Hayy, Hayy" produces a surge of livingness in the breath. It's in the sound itself.

## Life Beyond the Body

"All too often when we use the word living, we think living means being in a body with a beating heart. But really, embodied life is only one mode of life."

Life encompasses all planes:
- The subtle physical world
- The mental realm
- The angelic intelligences

"Life never dies. Death dies. Life lives. Life is innate, perpetual, primary."

## The Ground of Livingness

Looking deeper than eyesight and heartbeat, one can sink down and discover a ground of life more ancient than the novel manifestations we identify with. Our livingness in the One is inalienable. The mind might become oblivious to it, but the innateness persists.

"This is divine nature in its primal mode — before all of the names that describe the unfolding relationship between the whole and the parts. Sheer livingness."

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Guided Zikr Practice — Ya Hayyu
body:
# Guided Zikr Practice — Ya Hayyu

## The Practice

Invocation of the divine name Al-Hayy through its Ya-form: "Ya Hayyu" (O Living One).

Approximately 8 minutes of continuous group chanting, led by Pir Zia.

### Setup
- Class invokes the name together in unison
- Continuous repetition: "Ya Hayyu, Ya Hayyu, Ya Hayyu..."
- Duration: approximately 8 minutes [42:13 – 50:24]

### Closing Words

"The livingness in you is no different than the livingness in the Earth, which is no different than the eternal livingness which is in the divine being."

## Context

This is the first guided zikr practice of the Contemplation series, working with the first of the seven Ummahat (mother names). The practice moves from intellectual understanding of Al-Hayy into direct experiential invocation.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Chivalric Rule — Consider Your Responsibility Sacred
body:
# Chivalric Rule — Consider Your Responsibility Sacred

> **My conscientious self, consider your responsibility sacred.**

Each chivalric rule is itself a contemplation — reflecting upon our lives, our choices, seeing how our consciousness is in our own hands.

## The Teaching

We are free to choose our thoughts, words, and actions. We are free to sculpt our lives. We can't control other people, but we have perfect discretion in determining how we respond to them.

### What Are True Responsibilities?

Others project expectations in all directions constantly. The real question is: What is the **haqq** (right) that each being has? And what is the haqq of one's own self?

- We need to do justice to others in our relations with them
- At the same time, we need to do justice to ourselves
- Fundamentally: being true to your purpose, your **dharma**

"At the end of the day, we're not going to be asked about something that someone else was responsible for. We'll be asked about what we were responsible for."

### On Seriousness

Seriousness doesn't mean dry solemnity. There may be joy in it, lightness of touch. Seriousness means **sincerity, care, devoted attention**.

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Story of Yudhisthira and the Dog — The Dharma Within
body:
# Story of Yudhisthira and the Dog — The Dharma Within

## The Story (from the Mahabharata)

At the culmination of the Mahabharata, Yudhisthira and his family set out on a final pilgrimage to Mount Meru. Along the way, a black dog arrives and joins them. Through many ordeals, his brothers and Draupadi perish one by one. Yudhisthira buries each with solemn duty.

Near exhaustion, with only the dog remaining, a flying car descends from the sky. An angel offers to carry him to the summit — but the dog may not come.

Yudhisthira looks into the dog's eyes. The dog looks into his. He refuses: "I'm traveling with my friend."

The dog transforms, revealing its true nature: **"I am Dharma — and you were true to me."** Together they ascend to the summit.

## The Teaching

Dharma is linked with guidance, intuition, your own deepest knowing. It is not egoically defined — it has everything to do with relationships. Since it is of the spirit, an expression of divine guidance, it will never be simplistic or rigid. It is always an unfolding process of **being, knowing, doing**.

Not everything that captures our attention is dharmic. Much is incidental. Your purpose is a music that's unfolding — whatever notes glide into the space must be incorporated within the melody if they are to cohere meaningfully.

"Each little drop of negligence and distraction accumulates and soon we're drowning in forgetfulness."

## Cross-Traditional Reference

This Hindu epic teaching is used to illuminate the Sufi concept of sacred responsibility and the chivalric rule "Consider your responsibility sacred."

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan

title: Arabic and Sufi Terms — Contemplation Class 1
body:
# Arabic and Sufi Terms — Contemplation Class 1

| Arabic/Sufi Term | Meaning | Context/Significance |
|------------------|---------|----------------------|
| **Mujahada** | Striving, concentration | The stage of Concentration — learning stillness through effort |
| **Muraqabah** | Contemplation, watchfulness | The stage of Contemplation — the mind moves gracefully, purposefully |
| **Aman** | Safety, sanctuary, peace | The attitude that creates Dar al-Aman; arises from the interior life |
| **Dar al-Aman** | Realm of sanctuary | Built beginning in one's own heart through consideration for all beings |
| **Al-Asma as-Sab'a al-A'imma** | The Seven Leading Names | The seven mother names (Ummahat) — matrices from which the divine attributes unfold |
| **Ummahat** | Mothers, matrices | The seven leading divine names; each gives rise to the next |
| **Al-Hayy** | The Living | First of the seven mother names; sheer livingness, divine nature in its primal mode |
| **Ya Hayyu** | O Living One | Invocatory form of Al-Hayy used in zikr practice |
| **Jalali** | Majestic, powerful | Category of divine names associated with majesty and power |
| **Jamali** | Beautiful, graceful | Category of divine names associated with beauty and elegance |
| **Kamali** | Perfect, complete | Category where beauty and power merge |
| **Wazifa** | Assignment, daily practice | A divine name entrusted as a daily spiritual practice |
| **'Abd** | Servant | One who serves a divine name through Wazifa practice |
| **Haqq** | Right, truth, due | What is truly owed to each being; justice |
| **Dharma** | Sacred duty, purpose | One's true responsibility — always unfolding, never rigid |
| **Pir Dahan** | Mouth of the Pir | Place in California where Hazrat Inayat Khan meditated on a stone |
| **Silsila** | Chain (of transmission) | The lineage through which sacred names are transmitted |

---
*Kabir's Sufi Knowledge Base · Inayatiyya*
session: Suluk Academy 2026 Contemplation P1 Class 1 / March 27, 2026 / Pir Zia Inayat-Khan
